entry.title: Kickstart your Integrated Toolchain adoption with our new e-learning
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

We are thrilled to introduce our latest learning opportunity Integrated Toolchain E-Learning from the SAP Adoption Starter series.

What is the session about?

Dive into the essentials of the Integrated Toolchain with this e-learning course designed to accelerate your understanding and adoption. Covering the Integrated Toolchains setup and configuration requirements, you'll explore key SAP solutions—SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX, and SAP Cloud ALM—through dedicated sections that explain their features and how they contribute to the integrated toolchain. Finally, see it all come together in a live demonstration that showcases the toolchain’s functionality in action.

What are the session goals?

  • Understand the structure and purpose of the integrated toolchain and how its components work together.
  • Navigate SAP Cloud ALM, leverage its key features, and perform core administrative tasks.
  • Explore SAP Signavio, recognize its role in process management, and manage essential configurations.
  • Gain insight into SAP LeanIX for enterprise architecture management and configure its administrative settings.
  • Identify and set up the pre-requisites necessary to enable the integrated toolchain.
  • Watch a demonstration showcasing the toolchain’s capabilities in action.

    How to get in?

    If you haven't yet signed up for the SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value Maps, don't worry, we've got you covered! Get started with SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value Maps here. (Check out our step-by-step instructional video on how to join SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value maps.)

    Direct link to the new E-Learning session:

    Adoption Starter: Integrated Toolchain for Technical Administrators

    Do you need any additional help?

    Don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "Business Transformation Management" topic).

    Happy learning!

    Best regards,

    Rachel McDonagh

    Architect and Focus Advisor - Business Transformation Management Value Map

entry.title: General Data Splitter Tool for Optimized Efficient Data Processing and Scalable Integration
entry.content:
Struggling with handling massive data files in your integration flows? You're not alone! I'm Manaswini Puli , committed to break down complex integration challenges. In this hands-on guide, I'll walk you through using the General Splitter tool in SAP Integration Suite to efficiently break down large messages into manageable segments—boosting processing speed and scalability while handling structured XML/JSON data with ease.

Follow along as we transform complex data streams into optimized, scalable workflows step by step. If this solves your data bottleneck challenges, I'd love to hear your thoughts in the comments.

Below is the complete iFlow, featuring the use of a General Splitter to process structured messages effectively, along with OData integration for seamless data exchange.

Step-by-Step Guide: General Data Splitter Tool for Optimized, Efficient Data Processing and Scalable Integration

Step 1: Access Integration Suite

  1. Log in to SAP BTP Cockpit using your registered email and password.
  2. Go to your Subaccount and navigate to Subscriptions.
  3. Click on Integration Suite.

Step 2: Create a Package

  1. In Integration Suite, go to the Design tab and click Packages.
  2. Click Create Package, enter a valid name and description, then click Create.

Step 3: Create an Integration Flow

  1. Open the package you created.
  2. Click Create, select Integration Flow, enter a valid name and description, then click Create to open the editor.

Step 4: Configure HTTPS Sender Adapter

  1. Add an HTTPS sender to receive incoming data.
  2. Under the Connection tab:
    • Set the Address Path to /Products/Details.
    • Set Authorization to User Role.
    • Enter User Role: ESBMessaging.send.

Reason:

  • The Address Path defines the endpoint where the integration flow listens for incoming HTTPS requests, allowing external systems to send product data.
  • The ESBMessaging.send role ensures only authorized clients can access the endpoint.

Step 5: Add General Splitter

      1. Add  a General Splitter step after the HTTPS sender.

     2.  Under the Processing tab:

  • Set Expression Type to XPath.
    • Reason:The payload is in XML format, so setting the expression type to XPath enables precise navigation and extraction of relevant data elements.
  • Set XPath Expression to //productIdentifier.
    • Reason: productIdentifier is the key element in the payload that uniquely identifies each product, enabling extraction and individual processing.
  • Set Grouping to 1.
    • Grouping controls how many split messages are processed together.
    • Reason: The payload will be taking 3 product Identifiers, so the messages will be divided into 3 groups, each containing a single product Identifier for individual processing.

Step 6: Add Content Modifier

  1. Add a Content Modifier step after the HTTPS sender.
  2. Go to the Message Header section and click Add.
  3. Configure the header as follows:
    • Name: productIdentifier
    • Source Type: XPath
    • Source Value: //productIdentifier
    • Data Type: java.lang.String
  4. Reason: This extracts the productIdentifier value from the XML payload and stores it as a string in the message header for use in subsequent processing steps.

Step 7: Add OData Request-Reply

  1. Add an OData Request-Reply step after the General Splitter.
  2. In the Connection tab, configure the following:

Address:https://refapp-espm-ui-cf.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/espm-cloud-web/espm.svc

Reason: This OData endpoint is used to retrieve product data based on the productIdentifier stored in the message header.

          3.In the Processing tab, configure the following:

  • Set Operation Details to Query (GET) to retrieve data.
    Reason: Using the GET query operation allows fetching specific data from the OData service efficiently based on defined query parameters.
  • Click Select under Resource Path to specify the data source:

       Step 1: Connect to System

    Choose Remote as the connection source to connect to the external OData service.

     Step 2: Select Entity & Define Operation

    From the dropdown list, select the entity you want to query (for example, Products).
    After selecting the entity, specify the fields to optimize the data returned by the OData service.

       Step 3: Configure Filter & Sorting

    Under Filter By, set the filter condition:
    productId equals ${header.productIdentifier}

  • Click Finish to complete the configuration.

Step 8: Add Gather

  1. Add a Gather step at the end of the flow, after the OData Request-Reply.
  2. In the Processing tab, configure:
    • Incoming Format: XML
    • Aggregation Algorithm: Combine
      Reason: The input messages are in XML format, and the Combine algorithm joins them into one XML message. This helps gather all individual responses into a single output.

Step 9: Deploy and Test Integration Flow

  1. Deploy your integration flow from the editor.
  2. After deployment, get the endpoint URL generated for the HTTPS sender adapter.
  3. Open Postman to test the integration flow.
  4. Set the HTTP method to POST.
  5. Use the endpoint URL as the request URL.
  6. Under Authorization, select OAuth 2.0 and provide the following details from the service key created for the integration flow:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
    • Token URL

     7.In the Body tab, provide the XML payload or data you want to send.

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>

<root>

  <record>

    <Products>

      <productIdentifier>HT-2000</productIdentifier>

      <productIdentifier>HT-1138</productIdentifier>

      <productIdentifier>HT-1137</productIdentifier

    </Products>

  </record>

</root>

        8.Send the request and verify the response.

Step 10: Check the Processed Output

  • The input payload includes 3 productIdentifier entries.
  • With the General Splitter configured to group by 1, the message is split into 3 individual groups, each containing 1 product.
  • The following is Group 1, containing 1 productIdentifier as a result of the splitter configuration.

  • The following is  the OData response, which returns the product details for that specific productIdentifier.

  • At the Gather step, these messages are reassembled into a single message that contains all 3 processed product entries.

Disclaimer:
All the views and opinions in this blog are my own and are made in my personal capacity. SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: Issue Adding or Removing Proxies from Product in SAP API Management – Screen Keeps Loading
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m facing an issue in SAP API Management and would appreciate your help or insights.

I’ve created several API Proxies for my SAP CPI system and also created a Product. When I try to assign proxies to the Product:

  • For some proxies, it works fine, and I’m able to add them successfully.

  • For other proxies, the screen keeps loading indefinitely and never completes the assignment process.

Here’s what I’ve tried so far:

✅ I recreated and redeployed the problematic proxies.
✅ I tested the proxies directly in the browser — they work fine when called.
✅ I tried performing the assignment in Incognito mode and in different browsers.
✅ I even tried removing previously assigned proxies, but the UI again keeps loading endlessly during the remove operation.

Despite all this, the issue persists. It seems like there might be an internal problem in API Management, possibly some stuck metadata or backend inconsistency, but I’m not certain.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue?

  • Is there any workaround to force-remove a proxy from a Product (e.g. via REST APIs)?

  • Is there a way to clean up potentially stuck objects in API Management?

  • Any logs or places to check for deeper troubleshooting?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your support.

#

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) - Generate ESG Reports With AI Application
entry.content:
As part of the June 2025 release, SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) has introduced a new out-of-the-box functionality, Generate ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reports with AI application. With the Generate ESG Reports with AI app, the ESG reporting process is automated across the selected periods. It uses the ESG data that has already been imported into SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) and creates the reports based on the imported data. The AI generated reports are available to download in the .pdf format.

In this blog, I will outline how to define the content for custom templates based on set of facts, i.e.,  available metrics or reference facts, and how to generate ESG reports by leveraging AI.

Mini Note:

If you want to understand the nitty-gritty of SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) out-of-the-box functionalities and its Integration with SAP EHSM-EM (Emission Management), check-out my blog posts listed below.

  1. European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) 
  2. SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHS Environment Management 
  3. Addendum to SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHSM-EM 
  4. SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) Metric Management-Basic Custom Metric & Standard Custom Metric 
  5. Exporting and Importing Custom Objects and CPE Packages in SAP Sustainability Control Tower 
  6. Managing Qualitative Metrics in SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) for ESRS and BRSR Framework 
  7. SAP S/4HANA EHS : Environment Management (Emissions Management) – Part:1 (Master Data Entities) 
  8. SAP S/4HANA EHS : Environment Management (Emissions Management) – Part:2 (Identifying & Preparing Compliance Requirement) 
  9. SAP S/4HANA EHS : Environment Management (Emissions Management) – Part:3 (Compliance Scenario Activities) 

 

Let us get started...why wait?...

Generate ESG Reports With AI Application

 

Let us take look at the "Prerequisites" before we dive in

  1. ESG specific data must have been imported into SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT)
  2. Must have the roles, i.e., SUS_SCT_AI_Reporting_Assistant_Manager & SUS_SCT_AI_Reporting_Assistant_Viewer

ESG Data Imported into Sustainability Control Tower

 

For instance, I have created a template named "ESG Report Template for Calcination & Power"  to generate an ESG report for the above imported data using the AI application.

ESG Report Template for Calcination & Power Business Lines

 

let us use the "Create"  option to set-up a tailored custom template for ESG metrics and reference facts.  For Instance, I have added sections and subsections to align with the ESG pillars to have the template structure. We can rename, remove the sections and subsections at any point of time.

Sections and Subsections of ESG report

 

Now, let us start filling the sections with the content. To add the text to the "carbon emissions profile" , I have built the content by adding "Reference Facts" and " Metrics". We can add maximum 30 facts for each section or subsection.

Pillar: Environment

We can choose the "Reference facts" from the predefined facts, and metrics can be chosen from the available SAP provided quantitative metrics. The text  in this section will be generated by AI (Artificial Intelligence) for the metrics defined in the "Manage Metrics App".

Assignment of Reference Facts & Metrics to generate text for the Carbon Emissions Profile

 

Now, let us use the option " Generate" to preview the content for the above assigned reference facts and metrics.

 

AI generated text for the Carbon Emissions Profile

 

Now, let us generate charts for the "Carbon Emissions Profile". 

 

 

Following the successful assignment of the charts, use the option "Generate" charts for each metric.

 

AI generated graph for the GHG Emissions by GHG Scope & Business Locations

 

AI generated graph for the GHG Emissions by Business Country

 

AI generated graph for the Location & Market based Emissions

 

Now, let us create tables to fill out emissions metrics details by leveraging AI. Use the option " Add Table" to assign the required metrics tables.

For Instance, I have chosen two tables to represent  GHG Emissions (CO2e) & GHG Emissions Reduction Targets (CO2e).

 

 

Following the successful addition of the tables, use the option "Generate" to fill out the details for the selected table.

AI generated table for Scope-1 Emissions

 

AI generated table for Scope-2 & Scope-3 Emissions

 

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Energy Profile" under "Environmental" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the Energy Profile

 

AI generated tables for the Energy Profile

 

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Water Profile" under "Environmental" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the Water Profile

 

AI generated chart for Water Usage by Business Country

 

  AI generated chart for Water Usage Over Time

 

Pillar: Social

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Health & Safety" under the "Social" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the Health & Safety facts

 

AI generated graph for the work related incidents & fatalities

 

Pillar: Governance

In a similar manner, let us assign the reference facts and metrics to generate text , charts and tables for the "Governance Body" under the "Governance" pillar.

 

AI generated text for the governance body metrics

 

AI generated graph for the metric share of the women in management

 

AI generated graph for the Anti-Corruption Incidents metrics

 

Following the successful setup of the sections and subsections for the ESG reporting content, let us take a look at the configured report content preview. Let us use the option "Preview" to understand the structure of the ESG report.

 

 

Now, let us generate the ESG report  using the tailored custom template. 

 

Once the details have been provided to generate the report using the tailored custom template,  select the "Generate Report" option.

 

While the ESG report is being generated, system displays the progress of the report.

 

Once the system generates the report, its status is updated as "Completed".

Let us select the option "Download" to view the ESG report in Pdf format.

The ESG report has been downloaded in .pdf format.

 

 

Here we go....

AI generated ESG report - SAP SCT

 

 

😊 This brings me to the end of the process walk-through of the creation of the custom ESG template and AI usage to generate tailored ESG report for a specific sustainability reporting year. I hope my blog proves useful to the sustainability team. I look forward to leveraging AI capabilities at scale to strengthen ESG reporting structures.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: First smoke test after SAP LogServ solution deployment in Microsoft Sentinel - Part 0
entry.content:
Find your way to our central blog series entry here.

Dear community,

Due to the asynchronous integration between SAP LogServ and Microsoft Sentinel, it's advisable to perform a smoke test yourself before sharing your config data with SAP. This helps confirm if your receiving connector setup works and ensures the correct configuration will be shared with SAP the first time round.

 

Run a smoke test for ease of mind

Once the SAP LogServ push-connector is deployed on Microsoft Sentinel, you can start posting data towards its data collection endpoint. Use the provided REST client collection, or scripts to replicate the exact requests details that LogServ is performing too.

Maintain the variables as per your environment, see the LogServ sample payload and start posting. A working request shows http code 204 with no content on the response. See a sample output from the provided powershell script for reference. I prefer REST clients but people are different 😎

 

Find the parameters from the SAP LogServ Sentinel connector wizard on initial connect (see screenshot below). You can also get them directly from the individual resources. The data collection endpoint and data collection rule are created in the same resource group as your Log Analytics workspace underpinning Sentinel. They are prefixed with “SAPLogServ”. The app registration on Entra ID can be found from the Entra Application ID field.

 

Use the 2 KQL queries below to inspect your first data entry on the SAPLogServ_CL table in Sentinel.

SAPLogServ_CL
| take 10
SAPLogServ_CL
| summarize count() by clz_dir, clz_subdir
| order by clz_dir asc, clz_subdir asc

 

Wait a minute or two on first try. The population of new tables takes a short time initially.

 

Hurray you have a functioning data collection endpoint for the SAP LogServ data🎉

Now, continue your onboarding journey by sharing the config data with SAP over your chosen channel. Find the details on SAP's blog here.

What if you don’t see the data entry on the LogServ table?

  1. Verify the connection details: Did you really hit the right endpoint? Are you looking in the right tenant and log analytics workspace?
  2. Verify selection criteria: Is your Time range filter accurate? The smoke tests create entries for NOW or 30mins earlier in UTC.
  3. Did enough time pass on the initial table population? Consider a retry of the KQL query.
  4. Verify the Data collection rule has not been changed to filter all results or similar. See this blog part and KQL snippet for more details.

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap🌯. You saw today how to streamline your onboarding experience of SAP LogServ in Microsoft Sentinel. Simple smoke tests ensure a speedy onboarding and confidence in the shared setup configuration.

#Kudos to @Hemanth_Kusampudi and team for the partnership.

Get started with your deployment from here today.

Cheers Martin

entry.title: Discover the Updated Data Model Guide
entry.content:
Navigating data models in SAP SuccessFactors can be complex, but we’re making it easier, together. We’re excited to introduce the updated Data Model Guide, now featured in the Getting Started section of the Employee Central Help Portal page. This update is designed to help you and your teams quickly access the foundational information needed to configure and manage your SuccessFactors environment. Whether you're new to data models or refining an existing setup, this guide serves as a collaborative resource to align your efforts, reduce complexity, and accelerate implementation.

The updated data model guide is available on both the Employee Central and Platform pages. We recognized that users often had to consult multiple sources to understand field behaviors, localization requirements, and configuration dependencies. To address this, we enhanced the guide, ensuring easier findability and accessibility to the information you need. Each section that was added to the guide plays a vital role in helping users configure and manage their SAP SuccessFactors environments more effectively.

For more information, refer to the Setting Up Data Models and Managing Business Configuration guide. 

 

Data Models

Understand the different types of data models available and how they can be utilized effectively.

What’s Changed and Why:

We’ve restructured the guide to make it easier to understand the different types of data models in SAP SuccessFactors—Corporate, Succession, and Country/Region-Specific. This new layout offers a more intuitive and holistic view of how these models work together to shape your system’s structure.

These data models are the foundation for configuring Employee Central and other modules. They define everything from job structures to employee records. By including this section in the guide, we’re helping users see how each model contributes to the overall behavior of the system. This clarity leads to more accurate and efficient configurations, fewer errors, and a smoother implementation experience.

 

Foundation Objects

Learn about the essential building blocks that support your data structure.

What’s Changed and Why:

We’ve added Foundation Objects in the Data Model Guide. These are the core elements; like departments, locations, and job classifications, that define your organizational structure in SAP SuccessFactors.

The updated guide makes it easier to understand and configure. It gives you a clearer, more connected view of how the system is structured. Understanding this section helps you to dive deeper into how these objects function, how they relate to one another, and how they can be adapted for different countries and business units.

 

Business Configuration UI

Get acquainted with the user interface that facilitates seamless configuration management.

What’s Changed and Why:

We’ve added the Business Configuration UI (BCUI) guide to the Data Model Guide. This intuitive interface allows administrators to manage configuration settings directly, without needing to work with XML files. It’s designed to simplify the process and make configuration more accessible to a wider range of users.

Including BCUI in the Data Model Guide reflects our commitment to making configuration easier and faster. It empowers not just technical teams, but also HR professionals and business users who may not have a technical background. By placing BCUI within the broader data model context, users can better understand how their configuration choices impact the system’s structure and behavior, leading to more accurate and efficient setups.

 

Field Information

Access links to detailed field information specific to Employee Central fields, as well as the Country/Region-Specifics guide.

What’s Changed and Why:

This section links to detailed documentation on Employee Central fields as well as Country/Region-Specific configurations. It’s an essential reference for implementers who need to understand field behavior, dependencies, and localization requirements.

By embedding this information directly in the guide, it eliminates the need for external lookups, streamlines the configuration process, enhances usability, and supports faster, more consistent decision-making—ensuring that localization and configuration nuances are easily accessible.

 

Share Your Feedback


We value your input! As you explore the updated Data Model guide, you'll notice a link to a short survey. We’d be incredibly grateful if you could take a few minutes to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback plays a vital role in helping us continuously improve and tailor our resources to better support your needs.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4/HANA Cloud Public, J58: Financial close - Bala...
entry.content:
Hello,

I hope below solution might help you.

There are many different common reasons for Balance Validation Failure in SAP J58. Its pretty hard to reach to conclusion unless we debugg the whole process using trace.

However, below are the common causes:-

A. Imbalance in Debit and Credit Entries

Total debit ≠ total credit → This is the most typical reason.

Check: Line items entered, especially manual ones or those uploaded via interface.

B. Incorrect GL Account Configuration

GL accounts might be set incorrectly (e.g. only debit allowed, open item managed, etc.).

Check: Transaction code FS00.

C. Currency Difference

Amounts in local vs. document currency can mismatch.

Check: Currency fields in the document header and line items.

D. Incorrect Posting Key Usage

Using wrong posting keys (like 40 for debit and 50 for credit in FI).

Check: T-code OB41 for posting key configurations.

E. Rounding Issues

Decimal rounding issues in exchange rates can throw balance validation off by a small amount.

Check: Document currency amounts and exchange rate precision.

F. Custom Validation or BADI/BTE Check

Custom enhancements (e.g., via BADI_ACC_DOCUMENT, BTE, or user exit) may be rejecting the document.

Check: Debug the script or ask ABAP team to trace the enhancement point.

Try to troubleshoot further to know the exact error:-

1. Run J58 in Simulation Mode (if available)

Look at the detail report or logs for imbalance data.

2. Use T-code FB03 or FB50 to Manually Simulate the Entry

Try recreating the document manually to isolate the problem.

3. Enable SAP Trace

Ask your SAP technical team to run a trace (ST05 or SM12) during the J58 run.

4. Check Logs

If using Batch Input Session, check logs via SM35.

 

Try above steps and let me know. Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Milestone Billing for Sell-from-Stock and Drop Shipment Processes
entry.content:
You can now take advantage of milestone billing in the public cloud for two important sales processes:

  • Sell from stock
  • Third-party drop shipment

This new option became available with release 2508.

What is milestone billing?

With milestone billing, billing and delivery are no longer tied together. That means:

  • You can create an invoice right after the sales order, even before anything is delivered.
  • The billed quantity doesn’t have to match the delivered quantity right away.

Supported Scenarios

  • Sell from stock
    • Use item category CBAO
    • Revenue recognition key: SFSCCM (Completed Contract Method)
  • Third-party drop shipment
    • Use item categories DB1 or DB2
    • Also with revenue recognition key SFSCCM

This works with Billing Relevance I (Order-Related Billing – Billing Plan).

Limitation

If the system derives the revenue recognition key SFSCCM automatically, you can’t switch to another revenue recognition key, except to or from NONREV (Comprehensive non-revenue recognition key).

TECO and Completion

An order reaches TECO status when:

  • The ordered, delivered, and invoiced quantities match and
  • Delivery, order, and overall status = C (complete)

entry.title: Re: Profit Center segments in S/4HANA public cloud - SSCU...
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

A brief update confirming the solution for this question... 

As per the SSCUI excel spreadsheet provided in SAP note '3381770 - Delete entries from configuration activity (SSCUI)'

https://me.sap.com/notes/3381770/ESAP

you cannot delete Segments. 

For this particular implementation, the customer agreed that they only need one Segment per Co.Code (1110, 1120 and 1130).  We therefore amended the descriptions for 1000_A, 1000_B and 1000_C from: 

Segment A, Segment B and Segment C

to:

Segment 1110, Segment 1120 and Segment 1130

assigning these respectively to Profit Centers for the 3 Co.Codes. 

Kind regards,

Jules

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between Replicate Runtime Hier...
entry.content:
Hello @wangxlbest ,

I hope below solutuion might help you.

1. Verify Hierarchy in App “Manage Global Hierarchies”:

Go to Fiori App: Manage Global Hierarchies (App ID: F2913)

Search for hierarchy ID YPS2.

2. Confirm that it exists and is active.

Use Global Hierarchy Directly in Reports:

Since it's a global hierarchy now, reports like Contribution Margin Report or financial statement apps will read directly from the global hierarchy structure.

No replication is needed anymore.

3. Avoid Replication Job for Global Hierarchies:

Do not use J55 2.5.3 or similar runtime replication jobs for global hierarchies.

These are only applicable to non-migrated, legacy-style runtime hierarchies.

4. Update Documentation or Configuration Steps:

If you're following a SAP Best Practice or IMG Activity that includes replication, skip or adjust that step when the hierarchy has been migrated.

If issue still persists, I would encourage you to refer SAP Note 3355170 for solution to your issue.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

"Postings in account 6100110 (company XXXX) are allowed only in USD currency
Message No F5353

Diagnosis
The Co.Ge account "6100110" is managed in &V3. Registration in "EUR" is therefore not possible.

Procedure
If a gain or loss in exchange should be recorded, the foreign currency valuation function must be used.
Perform function
To avoid this, correct the entry or modify the "USD" split key in the Co.Ge. "6100110" personal account of the company "XXXX"."

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Posting Job Board Setup: eFinancial
entry.content:
Hello,

I know this is a question almost a year. 

Answering it though here if you haven't had the response before. Jobs and job boards configuration are not processed on Recruiting Posting preview environment. This is expected that some job board configurations cannot be configured and it is also expected that jobs are staying in "processing" status as 99% of the feed are not processed in preview. Only a couple of job boards have feeds in preview environment.

You can find those answers on the following KBAs : 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3245771/E

https://me.sap.com/notes/2721903/E

Valentin

entry.title: Dynamic Parameters - Windows 2022 server with Crystal Reports 2016 and Business Objects 4.2
entry.content:

We have set up a new QA environment on a Windows 2022 server with Crystal Reports 2016 and Business Objects 4.2. Is someone able to confirm that the dynamic parameters should work when creating the reports? Thanks.
